tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows significantly elevated creatinine in both serum and blood, indicating impaired renal function, and marked proteinuria and microalbuminuria, supporting possible nephropathy. Urine is abnormal in appearance and color, with low specific gravity. LDL cholesterol is elevated, increasing cardiovascular risk. HbA1c is unexpectedly low, possibly due to analytical or clinical factors. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation and further assessment for underlying renal disease and metabolic status.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
